tasks.title: Migrate docs/seeds/ files to seed-schema v3
tasks.description: All 4 seed files in docs/seeds/ were written before the v3 schema landed and use the old format: schema_version '2', a flat string source field, and no producer/status fields. The new seed-schema.json requires schema_version '3', a structured producer string, a source object, and a status field. Also, out_of_scope entries in some seeds are plain strings but the v3 schema requires {text, rationale} objects. Update all 4 seeds to conform to the new schema so validate-schema.sh passes on each one.
acceptance_criteria: bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh passes on all 4 files in docs/seeds/ with exit 0
Each seed has schema_version: '3', producer: 'to-seed', source: {kind: 'session', ref: null}, and a status field
Any out_of_scope entries are objects with text and rationale fields, not plain strings
Existing decisions, open_threads, and all other content are preserved unchanged

tasks.title: Migrate docs/tasks/ files to task-schema v2
tasks.description: Both task files in docs/tasks/ (20260603-1702 and 20260603-1846) were written with the old v1 format: they use a prd field and have no schema_version, producer, or source fields. The new task-schema.json requires schema_version '2', producer, and source, and forbids prd via additionalProperties: false. Update both files to conform: add schema_version '2', producer 'to-tasks', source {kind: 'session', ref: null} (both were generated from grill sessions, not from a seed or PRD file at the time they were created), and remove the prd field.
acceptance_criteria: bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh passes on both files in docs/tasks/ with exit 0
Both files have schema_version: '2', producer: 'to-tasks', source: {kind: 'session', ref: null}
prd field is removed from both files
All existing task statuses, IDs, titles, and acceptance criteria are preserved unchanged

tasks.title: Add source_branch to task-schema.json optional properties
tasks.description: to-e2e-tasks/SKILL.md instructs the model to add a source_branch top-level field to the task file it writes. But task-schema.json has additionalProperties: false and no source_branch property defined. Any e2e task file will fail schema validation. Fix by adding source_branch as an optional string property in task-schema.json. Do not add it to the required array. Update the to-e2e-tasks skill description if needed to match.
acceptance_criteria: task-schema.json has source_branch defined in its top-level properties as type string (optional, not required)
bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh passes on a task file that includes source_branch
bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh passes on a task file that omits source_branch
The embedded example in task-schema.json does not include source_branch (it remains a non-e2e example)

tasks.title: Fix skipped_reason description in runner-result-schema and contract
tasks.description: The skipped_reason field description in runner-result-schema.json says 'Explanation when status is warn. Null otherwise.' But the schema's own examples show skipped_reason as non-null for timeout ('Killed after 180s timeout') and deps-missing ('node_modules missing...') statuses. The description contradicts the examples. The runner-result-contract.md repeats the same wrong claim. Update both files: change the description to reflect that skipped_reason is non-null for warn, timeout, and deps-missing, and null otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: runner-result-schema.json skipped_reason description says non-null for warn/timeout/deps-missing, null for pass/fail
runner-result-contract.md skipped_reason documentation matches the schema description
bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh passes on runner-result-schema.json with exit 0 (examples still valid)

tasks.title: Add backward-compat guard to validate-schema.sh
tasks.description: validate-schema.sh changed its API in this PR: old form was 'validate-schema.sh <schema-path> <instance-path>' (validate an instance against a schema). New form is 'validate-schema.sh [--self-test] [schema-file...]' (validate schema files against embedded examples). When called with the old 2-arg form where both args are files, the script silently does the wrong thing: it treats both as schema files to self-validate, the instance file has no examples[], so it returns OK without ever checking the instance. Add a detection guard at the top of validate-schema.sh: if called with exactly 2 arguments and the first arg is an existing file (not a directory), print a clear error message explaining the API changed and exit non-zero. This surfaces the breakage instead of silently passing.
acceptance_criteria: Calling validate-schema.sh with 2 file args (old form) exits non-zero and prints a message explaining the API changed
Calling validate-schema.sh with 0 args (validate all contracts/) still works correctly
Calling validate-schema.sh with 1 schema file arg still works correctly
Calling validate-schema.sh --self-test still works correctly
bash ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/tests/test-validate-schema.sh passes if it exists

tasks.title: Fix grep regex in debug SKILL.md FU-001 step
tasks.description: The FU-001 follow-up step in debug/SKILL.md reads: 'Run: grep -r '[DEBUG-' .' The unescaped [ opens a regex character class, so grep matches any character in {D, E, B, U, G, -} rather than the literal string '[DEBUG-'. Debug instrumentation tagged [DEBUG-...] would not be found. Change the step to: 'Run: grep -r '\[DEBUG-' .' so the bracket is escaped and the literal prefix is matched.
acceptance_criteria: debug/SKILL.md FU-001 step uses grep -r '\[DEBUG-' not grep -r '[DEBUG-'
Running grep -r '\[DEBUG-' . in a test directory containing a file with the string '[DEBUG-foo]' returns that file
